description:
## Goal
Bring the interp leg to parity with JIT/AOT for list-cons destructure. Today `fz interp` on `[h | t] = xs` fails with `interp .5.2: prim Discriminant(11) not yet supported (lands in fz-ul4.23.5.3+)`.

## Independence
This is independent of T1/T2 and can land in parallel — it's a pure interp gap.

## Scope
- Implement the `Discriminant` primitive in `src/ir_interp.rs` for list values (distinguishes `[]` from `[h|t]`).
- Mirror the semantics used by JIT/AOT — same prim, same dispatch.
- No changes to lowering, typer, or codegen.

## Acceptance probe
```
fn main() do
  [h | t] = [10, 20, 30]
  print(h)
  print(t)
end
```
`fz interp` produces the same output as `fz run` and `fz build`.

Also: a probe on `[h | t] = []` runtime-fails with `match_error` in interp the same way it would in JIT/AOT (BEAM-conform).

## Done when
- `Discriminant` for list values works in `ir_interp`.
- The fixture-matrix legs no longer skip/fail interp for list-cons destructure.
- All tests pass.
title: T3: interp parity — list-cons Discriminant
